hypothesis: Adaptive diversity constraints computed from 3D-conformer similarity (e.g., mean pairwise heavy-atom RMSD and 3D shape fingerprints) and applied to graph- or diffusion-based generative models will preserve significantly greater stereochemical and conformational diversity—quantified by higher mean pairwise RMSD and a larger number of 3D conformer clusters—while achieving equal or better property optimization scores after 1000 training iterations than equivalent adaptive constraints based on 2D fingerprints or SMILES n-grams.
